chapter_summary: This portion of Gheranda Samhita Chapter 1 (GhS_1.22-1.44) continues the shatkarma purifications of ghatastha yoga. It completes the secret antar-dhauti by prescribing prakshalana of the abdominal shakti-nadi performed standing navel-deep in water, declared supremely secret (para gopya) and unattainable even by the gods, said to grant a divine body (devadeha) yet possible only after one has mastered the ardha-dharana retention. It then teaches Danta-dhauti, the cleansing of five seats: the teeth-root, tongue-root, the two ear-cavities, and the frontal aperture. The tongue (lambika) is rubbed, milked with butter and drawn out with an iron instrument until it lengthens, conquering ageing, death and disease. Rubbing the ear-cavities makes the inner sound (nada) manifest, and pressing the frontal aperture clears kapha so the nadi purifies and divine sight (divyadrishti) arises. Hrd-dhauti (heart/chest cleansing) is given in three modes -- by reed-stick (danda), by vomiting water (vamana), and by swallowing cloth (vaso) -- destroying heart disease, gulma, fever, spleen, leprosy and the kapha-pitta humours. Finally Mula-shodhana cleanses the root with stick or finger and water, since until the root is cleansed the apana stays harsh; this softens the bowels, cures indigestion, and kindles the digestive fire (vahni-mandala) that brings radiance and strength day by day.

teachings.teaching: The shakti-nadi prakshalana, washed navel-deep in water, is supremely secret (para gopya), unattainable even by the gods, and by mere cleansing the body becomes a divine body (devadeha).
teachings.lens_note: For Sharma the secrecy IS transmission: the deepest pranic purification comes only through the guru-given practice that works the shakti-nadi through which kundalini rises. Devadeha is read literally -- the vessel transmuted toward the deathless light-body, not a metaphor.

teachings.teaching: Maha-dhauti does not arise in one who has not first mastered the ardha-dharana retention capacity; purification follows a strict order of prerequisites.
teachings.lens_note: Sharma's krama principle: no opening comes by skipping steps. An unprepared vessel yields nothing, and capacity in retention is the gate to the deeper cleansings.

teachings.teaching: Danta-dhauti cleanses five seats -- teeth-root, tongue-root, the two ear-cavities and the frontal aperture -- and is supreme because these serve dharana and must be kept pure daily.
teachings.lens_note: Sharma reads the five head-gates as apertures of dharana, cleansed because a fouled gate cannot hold prana; the teeth and tongue-root border khechari territory where the higher locks are formed.

teachings.teaching: Rubbing, milking with butter, and drawing the tongue out with an iron instrument lengthens the lambika and conquers ageing, death and disease.
teachings.lens_note: This is the physical groundwork of khechari mudra: the tongue lengthened to seal the cranial aperture and reach the nectar (amrita), the closed gate stopping it being consumed by the gastric fire so decay halts.

teachings.teaching: Rubbing the ear-cavities makes the inner sound (nada) manifest, and pressing the frontal aperture clears kapha so the nadi purifies and divine sight (divyadrishti) arises.
teachings.lens_note: For Sharma these are the doorways of laya yoga -- anahata nada and ajna divyadrishti -- where a gross cleansing precipitates subtle perception; physical shuddhi is the precondition for the divine senses, not a substitute for them.

teachings.teaching: Hrd-dhauti is threefold -- by reed-stick (danda), by vomiting water (vamana), by swallowing cloth (vaso) -- and destroys heart disease, gulma, fever, spleen, leprosy and the kapha-pitta humours.
teachings.lens_note: Sharma reads heart cleansing as clearing the anahata seat where prana stagnates; removing kapha-pitta lifts the doshic veil that blocks prana from rising past the heart toward the higher centres.

teachings.teaching: Until the root (mula) is cleansed, apana remains harsh (apana-krurata); therefore root cleansing must be done with every effort, softening the bowels, curing indigestion, and kindling the digestive fire.
teachings.lens_note: The lens keystone: the entire ascent depends on the apana at muladhara being reversed and merged with prana to awaken the fire that lifts shakti. Kindling the vahni-mandala is the first cause of the upward process -- nothing rises until the bottom is clean.
